title: Amylotheca duthieana
taxonomy: Amylotheca duthieana is a species of plant in the family Actinidiaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and parts of southern Asia. The plant is classified as a flowering plant, with its scientific name Amylotheca duthieana first described in 1912. It belongs to the order Ericales and the family Actinidiaceae, which consists of a group of plants commonly known as the bittershrub family. Amylotheca duthieana is also sometimes referred to as the Duthie's staff ivy.
uses: Amylotheca duthieana, also known as Duthiea, is a plant species with reported medicinal uses, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties.
